value.Title: IKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGN-S093-S3VB-54H1-00000-00IKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: DYWIDAG Group (DYWIDAG Or The Group Or The Company ) Final Results

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-FPP3-SDK3-446H-00000-00DYWIDAG Group (DYWIDAG Or The Group Or The Company) Final Results2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisDYWIDAG Group (DYWIDAG Or The Group Or The Company) Final Results

Strong financial performance and record order bookMunich, Germany, May 01, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- DYWIDAG, a specialist engineering business providing products and services to stabilize ground; create, strengthen and support concrete structures; and monitor the structural health of critical infrastructure, announces its final results for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("the period").


Financial Highlights
· A strong financial performance with revenue, cash balances and adjusted operating profit all ahead of prior year.
· Group revenue increased 2.6% to €393.4m (FY24: €382.5m)
· Group adjusted operating profit increased by 9.4% to €23.6m (FY24: · Group order book increased to record levels of €364m (FY24: €241m) underpinned by long-term framework positions, particularly in the renewables sector providing post-tensioning services for the construction of wind towers.
· During the year the group replaced its existing finance facilities with finance raised from a 90m Nordic Bond.
· Cash generation and Net cash balances of €42.5m (FY2024: €29.6) were above expectations.
· The decrease in Reported Operating Result vs 2024 was caused by one off costs incurred in the course of 2025 relating to re-financing, investment in new ERP systems and re-structuring our US operations. The investment in our US operations will yield significant benefits in 2026.
Operational Highlights
. HSE performance was maintained in terms of lost time incident rates and improved in terms of incident frequency rates. We continue to implement our Zero Harm program.
. Revenues and profitability increased driven by strong performance in Europe and high activity in installing post tensioning in Wind Towers.
. 200m+ of term contracts were secured in the Wind sector and 15m of LNG projects delivering a record order intake and back log.
. Gordie Howe International Bridge and Corpus Christi Harbor Bridge stay-cable projects were successfully completed.
. A new manufacturing facility was established in Pennsylvania in the USA.
. In Q4 2025, new senior leadership was appointed in the US and UK to strengthen our existing management teams.
. In December, the divestment of our loss-making concrete technology product business in France - TAM Groupe - was concluded resulting in a non-cash impairment but reducing our lease and other debt/debt-like items.
Outlook
. Record order backlog should underpin revenues in 2026.
. Clear visibility of material new growth opportunities in renewables, repair and strengthening and energy transmission markets.
. Our global geographic footprint, exposure to different sectors and service line mix should continue to ensure financial resilience despite the impact of the Iran war.
. A number of potential M&A targets have been identified which would fit with the group's strategy of focussing on its core service lines and enhance earnings.
Hugh Pelham, CEO of DYWIDAG, commented:
" Our implementation of key strategic growth initiatives in the renewable energy sector is on track. We have now secured over 240m of contracts to provide post-tensioning services for hybrid wind towers with key customers in Europe and have significant bid opportunities in Saudi Arabia and Asia in the renewables sector. In 2025, we secured 20m of work on 5 LNG tanks that are being constructed in the USA, Germany and Taiwan.
We have established a new manufacturing facility / distribution centre in the USA, Pennsylvania, which commenced operating in Q4 and will enable growth in North America. We have also launched new monitoring products in the UK rail sector.
Our management teams have been strengthened in the USA and the UK with the appointment of new senior leadership. Operationally, we continue to improve delivery times and have maintained a low safety incident rate. We have successfully completed two major stay-cable projects in the US/Canada – Gordie Howe International Bridge and Corpus Christi Harbor Bridge (the largest stay-cable bridge in the US), and the Dan Jiang stay-cable bridge in Taiwan (the largest single tower stay-cable bridge in the world).
We are executing on our strategy of focusing on our core business and have completed the sale of our non-core loss making subsidiary in France - TAM Groupe. The divestment has resulted in reduced lease, employment-related and factoring liabilities on the Group balance sheet. We have reached settlement on two significant historic contract disputes in the US relating to projects started in 2018/19 and that were materially completed in 2022. I am confident the positive momentum will continue in 2026."
For further information, please contact:
DYWIDAG Group
Hugh Pelham, Chief Executive Officer... +49 89309 050100
Piotr Peczak, Chief Financial Officer
About DYWIDAG Group
· DYWIDAG is a specialist engineering business providing products and services to stabilize ground and to construct or strengthen concrete or steel structures. The company also provides infrastructure monitoring services.
· The business was founded in 1865 and is headquartered in Munich.
· The main service lines are post-tensioning systems, geotechnical products for ground support, stay cables systems and infrastructure monitoring solutions.
· Key customers are companies in the infrastructure, energy, and residential/commercial building industries.
For more information, please visit the DYWIDAG Group website:

urn:contentItem:6JK3-WT63-RSWV-8196-00000-00IKEA GROUP - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W1PK-00000-00IKEA GROUP - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WEEKLY BUSINESS AFFAIRS SNAPSHOT: THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S0GM-T0NH-00000-00WEEKLY BUSINESS AFFAIRS SNAPSHOT: THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisWEEKLY BUSINESS AFFAIRS SNAPSHOT: THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY [10 Press Releases]

+ PENN STATE COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURAL SCIENCES COMMEMORATES YOUTH FOOD LAB ANNIVERSARY WITH FAO VISIT

UNIVERSITY PARK, Pa. - Penn State's College of Agricultural Sciences hosted a delegation from the FAO to mark the first anniversary of the World Food Forum Youth Food Lab North America at Penn State. The event focused on expanding collaboration on global agrifood systems, innovation and education. Behring, assistant dean and director of Ag Sciences Global, noted that the partnership rests on ongoing engagement in food security research, teaching and outreach.

+ BERDANIER AWARDED BRYANT EARLY CAREER PROFESSORSHIP IN MECHANICAL ENGINEERING

UNIVERSITY PARK, Pa. Reid Berdanier, associate professor of mechanical engineering at Pennsylvania State University, has been named the Bryant Early Career Professor. The three-year appointment runs through 2028. He becomes the eighth current faculty member to hold a named early-career professorship. According to Mary Frecker, head of the Department of Mechanical Engineering, the recipients are leading researchers in healthcare, transportation systems, renewable energy storage and more.

+ IST FACULTY MEMBER DONGWON LEE AWARDED FELLOWSHIP FROM CAIS TO EXPLORE CLAIM-MAKING AND AI-BASED DETECTION

Dongwon Lee, a Penn State IST professor, has earned a fellowship at CAIS in Bochum, Germany. He will study how misinformation spreads globally and how AI can assist in identifying it more quickly. CAIS supports research on the societal opportunities and challenges of digital transformation and will host Lee from July through September.

+ PENN STATE EMPHASIZES MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT IN EMPLOYEE BENEFITS PACKAGE

Editor's note: This story is part of a series designed to help employees better understand and navigate their benefits. University Park, Pa. - During Mental Health Awareness Month this May, Penn State Human Resources is reminding employees about how their benefits and resources can support mental health and well-being. Jill Musser, senior director of employee benefits, said the university recognizes the hard work of staff who balance work with family and community commitments, and that access to resources to maintain balance and manage stress is a key part of the effort to support mental health. The university's commitment is to foster a culture of physical and mental health and well-being where everyone feels valued and supported.

+ PRESCHOOL STRESS REGULATION LINKED TO PARENTING QUALITY, PENN STATE STUDY FINDS

A Penn State study ties aggressive parenting to poorer development of stress regulation in preschoolers. Led by Jianing Sun and Erika Lunkenheimer, the study, published in Child Development, supports the idea that parents are the main physiological regulators for young kids. It highlights the "co-regulation" concept, noting that a parent's calm state helps a child modulate their stress response, while findings show parental influence remains strong in the preschool years.

+ PENN STATE'S SUSTAINABLE LABS PROGRAM WRAPS FOURTH YEAR, HIGHLIGHTING CARBON SAVINGS AND STUDENT ENGAGEMENT

The Penn State Sustainable Labs Program has completed its fourth year, expanding its reach across campuses while advancing the drive to reduce the environmental footprint of research spaces. The 2025-26 cohort included 20 labs across multiple colleges, with seven labs recertifying after the initial 2022-23 run. Participation remains free, and the program emphasizes student engagement through its Sustainable Lab Consultant model, pairing students with labs to implement sustainable practices. The year also saw My Green Lab 2.0, a more responsive platform with expanded tools to track progress; labs reported saving over $100,000 and reducing about 500 metric tons of CO2e through behavior-based changes.

+ IST FACULTY MEMBER AT PENN STATE RECEIVES FELLOWSHIP TO STUDY MISINFORMATION AND FACT-CHECKING PRACTICES WORLDWIDE

Dongwon Lee, a professor at Penn State's College of Information Sciences and Technology, has received a CAIS fellowship in Bochum, Germany, to study misinformation globally and the use of artificial intelligence to speed up spotting false information online. CAIS funds research on the societal opportunities and challenges of digital transformation and will host Lee from July through September. He is interim head of IST's Department of Privacy and Cybersecurity Informatics and a faculty affiliate at Penn State's Center for Socially Responsible Artificial Intelligence. He noted that collaborations in Germany and across Europe could provide access to European fact-checking datasets and help generate new research questions. Lee will use a collection of about 200,000 fact-checked claims from around the world, written in 39 languages, to study how false information spreads and how people verify it. He aims to answer two main questions.

+ EIGHT CURRENT FACULTY HOLD NAMED EARLY-CAREER PROFESSORSHIPS IN PENN STATE MECHANICAL ENGINEERING

Reid Berdanier, associate professor of mechanical engineering, has been awarded the Bryant Early Career Professorship. The three-year appointment runs through 2028, and he is the department’s eighth current holder of a named early-career professorship. Mary Frecker notes that recipients work across healthcare, transportation systems, and renewable energy storage.

+ PENN STATE'S COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURAL SCIENCES DEEPENS TIES WITH FAO ON YOUTH FOOD LAB MILESTONE

Penn State's College of Agricultural Sciences welcomed a delegation from the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations to mark the one-year anniversary of the World Food Forum Youth Food Lab North America at Penn State partnership and to explore deeper collaboration on global agrifood systems, innovation and education. Deanna Behring, assistant dean and director of Ag Sciences Global, noted that the partnership builds on a long-standing mission dating back to 1961 and the U.S. Foreign Assistance Act, aimed at advancing food security and agricultural development globally. "This new step in our partnership with FAO builds on our previous engagement by bringing new energy and innovation to areas critical to both our missions." The delegation's visit included discussions with Penn State leaders and students, including FAO deputy director-general Beth Bechdol, Lauren Phillips, director of partnerships and UN collaboration, and Jocelyn Brown Hall, director of the Liaison Office for North America, about global agrifood systems and workforce development.

+ WE ARE! SPOTLIGHT CELEBRATES 22 CAMPUS CONTRIBUTORS — MAY 11

In its weekly We Are! feature, Penn State recognizes 22 staff members who have gone above and beyond in service to the University. This week we highlight four colleagues whose peers note their leadership and teamwork: Sarah Davis, educator for food, families, and communities in Penn State Extension, is praised for delivering strong programs and providing leadership, with attentive listening, clear communication, and a focus on improving internal processes. Sarah DeWolfe, assistant director of financial coaching in the Sokolov-Miller Family Financial and Life Skills Center, is described as reliable, knowledgeable, and collaborative, stepping into interim roles when needed and supporting the team. Michele Duffey, associate teaching professor of kinesiology in the College of Health and Human Development, is celebrated for listening to diverse voices, solving problems, and advancing student success through cross-campus collaboration. Lisa Edwards, academic adviser in Smeal College of Business, is recognized for creating a welcoming office atmosphere, maintaining a positive attitude, and delivering compassionate care to students. Their efforts reflect the Penn State commitment to service and teamwork that defines the We Are! tradition.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHER PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-YDG3-RRJ6-N2F5-00000-00IKEA GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WEEKLY CORPORATE BRIEFING: THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-3853-RVJC-C2CH-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE BRIEFING: THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE BRIEFING: THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ THE PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIVERSITY [10 Press Releases]

+ PENN STATE EXPANDS SUSTAINABLE LABS PROGRAM AS MY GREEN LAB 2.0 ROLLS OUT ACROSS CAMPUSES

The Penn State Sustainable Labs Program recently concluded its fourth year, expanding its footprint among campuses and strengthening efforts to make research spaces more sustainable. The 2025-26 cohort comprised 20 labs spanning several colleges, with seven labs recertifying after participating in the program's first year. Participation remains free, and the program supports teams in adopting sustainable practices without compromising scientific discovery. New to this year was My Green Lab's updated 2.0 platform, offering a more responsive interface and expanded tools to track progress. Labs reported savings of more than $100,000 and a reduction of roughly 500 metric tons of CO2e through behavior changes. Rumery noted the platform's impact estimator helped quantify changes already underway. As always, the Sustainable Lab Consultant model keeps students at the center, working directly with labs to install sustainable practices and help transform research culture.

+ PENN STATE IST RESEARCHER SECURES CAIS FELLOWSHIP TO ANALYZE CLAIM-MAKING AND VERIFICATION ACROSS BORDERS

Dongwon Lee, a professor in Penn State's College of Information Sciences and Technology, has been awarded a fellowship at the Center for Advanced Internet Studies (CAIS) in Bochum, Germany. He will study how misinformation spreads globally and how artificial intelligence can help make spotting false information online faster and easier. CAIS, which funds research on the societal opportunities and challenges of digital transformation, will host Lee from July through September. He is interim head of IST's Department of Privacy and Cybersecurity Informatics and a faculty affiliate at Penn State's Center for Socially Responsible Artificial Intelligence. To maintain a trustworthy and healthy digital ecosystem, Lee said, 'To maintain a trustworthy and healthy digital ecosystem, it is critically important to improve our understanding of global patterns of fact-checking, especially under the growing influence of generative AI and AI hallucinations.' 'Through the new collaborations I hope to establish in Germany and across Europe, I aim to gain access to new European fact-checking datasets and develop novel research questions.' Lee will work with a collection of about 200,000 fact-checked claims from around the world, written in 39 languages, to study how false or misleading information spreads and how people verify it. He aims to answer two main questions.

+ BERDANIER EARNS BRYANT EARLY CAREER PROFESSORSHIP, EIGHTH RECIPIENT IN THE DEPARTMENT

Reid Berdanier, an associate professor of mechanical engineering, has been named the Bryant Early Career Professorship. The three-year appointment runs through 2028, placing Berdanier among the department's rising researchers. He becomes the eighth current faculty member to hold a named early-career professorship in mechanical engineering. Mary Frecker, head of the department, notes that recipients work across healthcare, transportation systems, and renewable energy storage.

+ FAO AND PENN STATE CELEBRATE MILESTONE OF WORLD FOOD FORUM YOUTH FOOD LAB NORTH AMERICA COLLABORATION

Penn State's College of Agricultural Sciences hosted a delegation from the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations at University Park to commemorate the one-year anniversary of the World Food Forum Youth Food Lab North America at Penn State partnership and to discuss expanding collaboration on global agrifood systems, innovation and education. The visit highlighted a growing partnership built on engagement in food security research, teaching and outreach, according to Deanna Behring, assistant dean and director of Ag Sciences Global. During FAO's visit, Penn State leadership, faculty and students engaged with Beth Bechdol, FAO deputy director-general; Lauren Phillips, director of partnerships and UN collaboration; and Jocelyn Brown Hall, director of the Liaison Office for North America, along with other delegation members, during a day focused on global agrifood systems, innovation and workforce development. Behring emphasized the college's mission as a land-grant institution and how the new steps in collaboration with FAO bring renewed energy and innovation to areas central to both organizations' missions.

+ PENN STATE SALUTES 22 STAFF IN WE ARE! TRIBUTE — MAY 11

As part of our regular "We Are!" feature, Penn State recognizes 22 Penn Staters who have gone above and beyond in their work. This week we celebrate four colleagues singled out by peers for their leadership and teamwork: Sarah Davis, educator for food, families, and communities in Penn State Extension, is noted for delivering excellent programs and providing strong program leadership; she listens to concerns, explains challenges, and advocates for colleagues while continually improving internal processes. Sarah DeWolfe, assistant director of financial coaching in the Sokolov-Miller Family Financial and Life Skills Center, is described as knowledgeable and capable, always willing to support colleagues. When leaves occur, she steps into interim roles and remains a true team player. Michele Duffey, associate teaching professor of kinesiology in the College of Health and Human Development, goes above and beyond in every role by listening to all voices, thinking through problems, and collaborating across the University to keep student interests central. Lisa Edwards, academic adviser in Smeal College of Business, is recognized for stepping up when help is needed and for being a welcoming presence in the office. Her positive attitude and care for students have a lasting impact. The four honorees exemplify the Penn State community’s spirit of service, and the We Are! celebration continues thanks to their dedication.

+ CLIMAVISION AND PENN STATE COLLABORATE TO EXPAND WEATHER RESEARCH AND EDUCATION

Penn State and Climavision have formed a new collaboration to enhance weather research and education, giving students and faculty access to advanced radar data and expertise. Under the partnership, Climavision will grant Penn State access to its high-resolution radar network to supplement the existing National Weather Service coverage in areas that are undersampled. Data will be provided in real time and historically, enabling a wide range of classroom, research and applied learning activities. Climavision's scientists will also deliver lectures and training to the Penn State community."The Climavision radar data will potentially be a treasure trove for our students with opportunities afforded for both research and classroom activities," Markowski said. Climavision's data supports analyses of severe weather, precipitation, lake-effect snow, smoke plumes and other atmospheric conditions, and the company's network is designed to improve low-level observations where public systems have limited coverage. The company estimates about 4 million people in Pennsylvania live in these coverage gaps.

+ CONTROLLED BURNS SHOW UNEVEN EFFECTIVENESS AGAINST TWO AGGRESSIVE INVASIVE SHRUBS, PENN STATE STUDY FINDS

Invasive woody plants are a major ecological challenge in eastern U.S. forests, outcompeting native species and forming dense thickets that crowd native plants and disrupt ecosystems. Land managers have used prescribed fire with some success to kill them, but effectiveness remains debated. Penn State researchers assessed three different experimental burning regimens on burning bush and border privet, two of the most aggressive invaders across North American landscapes. Their results were mixed, underscoring that prescribed fire may not consistently eradicate these shrubs. The team recently published the findings in Ecosphere.

+ PENN STATE EXPERT OUTLINES TICK RISKS AND PROTECTIONS FOR PEOPLE, PETS, LIVESTOCK

UNIVERSITY PARK, Pa. - May is Lyme Disease Awareness Month, highlighting Pennsylvania's status as a top state for Lyme disease and other tick-borne illnesses. Penn State experts say there are practical steps to protect people, pets and livestock from ticks and the pathogens they carry. Erika Machtinger, associate professor of entomology in Penn State's College of Agricultural Sciences, specializes in ticks and other arthropod pests and the risks they pose to humans and animals. There are more than 900 tick species worldwide, with at least 25 identified in Pennsylvania. Two species account for more than 90% of the tick-identification requests sent to Penn State: the blacklegged tick and the American dog tick. Three additional species are found in Pennsylvania: the lone star tick, the Asian longhorn tick and the winter tick.

+ AI IN GLOBAL HEALTH: COULD DEMOCRATIZATION BE UNDERMINED BY PERSISTING GAPS, PENN STATE EXPERT SAYS

UNIVERSITY PARK, Pa.—Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the tools used in global health decision-making, including modeling for disease-control policies, financing decisions, and vaccination strategies. Matt Ferrari, a Penn State biology professor and director of the Center for Infectious Disease Dynamics, says these advances create opportunities for more sophisticated modeling while also risking the entrenchment of existing disparities. Ferrari notes the field stands to gain from AI-driven analyses that can better inform policy choices and resource allocation."There's an irrational optimism that AI, machine learning and computational tools are going to democratize access to science and policy influence," Ferrari said. "AI, if treated naively, will likely reinforce existing inequities so we should be proactive about avoiding that."The scholar emphasizes that success depends on deliberate design: making sure models are accessible, transparent and inclusive, with attention to who benefits from the decision-making tools. Penn State researchers say the potential is there to expand global health capacity, but only if equity is baked into AI development and deployment.

+ PENN STATE TEAM TOPS MID-ATLANTIC CHEM-E-CAR CONTEST, QUALIFIES FOR INTERNATIONAL CHAMPIONSHIP

UNIVERSITY PARK, Pa. - Penn State's Chem-E-Car team, which competes under the AIChE student chapter, won the Chem-E-Car Performance Competition at the 2026 AIChE Mid-Atlantic Conference, finishing ahead of 21 other schools and earning a place in the 2026 Annual Student Conference, an international event set for November. The Mid-Atlantic Conference serves as a regional qualifier for the global event, bringing together the strongest student teams from the region as they prepare for the international finals later in the year. Last year, Penn State's Chem-E-Car team finished fourth in the world competition, a benchmark the group has used to push for continued improvement."From the time I joined to now, we have all learned a lot, worked hard and made many improvements to our approach to developing each car," said Meilisa Thomas, the club's president and a fourth-year chemical engineering student. "I see these recent successes as an indication that we're heading in the right direction."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - UPDATES FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-8BN3-S2WW-G55Y-00000-00IKEA GROUP - UPDATES FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - UPDATES FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
